title: British politics ten years after Brexit – European Council on Foreign Relations
gist: A podcast teaser (show notes only) for an ECFR episode in which Mark Leonard interviews Labour strategist Marcus Roberts on how UK public attitudes to Brexit have shifted a decade on, against a backdrop of Keir Starmer's resignation after losing to Andy Burnham in the Makerfield by-election.

insights.claim: Keir Starmer has resigned as UK prime minister after Labour rival Andy Burnham's decisive victory in the Makerfield by-election.
insights.speaker: Mark Leonard, Marcus Roberts
insights.so_what: A leadership change driven by an intra-Labour challenger signals fresh direction risk for UK policy—watch Burnham's stance on Europe as the new pivot.
insights.quote: Keir Starmer has resigned as prime minister after his Labour rival Andy Burnham’s decisive victory in the Makerfield by-election.
insights.locator: It is ten years since Britain’s decision

insights.claim: New ECFR research finds UK public views on Brexit have evolved significantly even though much of the political debate is still rooted in the divisions of 2016.
insights.speaker: Mark Leonard, Marcus Roberts
insights.so_what: Confirms a voter-vs-political-class gap on Europe—an underpriced driver of future UK-EU policy shifts.
insights.quote: public opinion is changing in ways that many politicians have failed to recognise

insights.claim: ECFR's accompanying policy brief is titled 'Brexit isn't working: British voters are ready for a European future', framing voters as primed for re-engagement with Europe.
insights.speaker: Mark Leonard, Marcus Roberts
insights.so_what: The explicit 'European future' framing flags institutional momentum toward UK-EU rapprochement as a multi-year theme for UK assets.
insights.quote: Brexit isn’t working: British voters are ready for a European future
